title: records.jsonl — one attempted sample per line
description: The records half of a run-set: `records.jsonl` holds one instance of this schema per line, one line per ATTEMPTED sample (the Rust definition is `arm_harness::evidence::RunRecord` and this schema mirrors it field for field). Every attempted sample gets a record, including ones that failed — there is no way to express 'this sample did not work out' by omitting it, because omission is exactly what the totality check catches (docs/ARM-ALTRA.md §Evidence integrity #6: a missing sample is a failure to account, not a pass). These records are the ONLY numbers a floor checker may use; the manifest carries no totals to believe. UNTESTED ON SILICON.

sample_id.description: Dense index into the attempted samples, `0..attempted`. The totality check is exactly: these are present, once each, with no gaps.

seed.description: The seed the params page carried. The checker re-derives the expected count from `(payload, scale, seed)` — it never reads a predicted count out of the evidence.

trips.description: The trip count the payload was given. `oracle_model::trips(payload, scale)` is the single definition; this field records what the harness actually passed.

work_begin.description: `BR_RETIRED` sampled at the window's opening mark (the MMIO store of `MARK_BEGIN`).

work_end.description: `BR_RETIRED` sampled at the window's closing mark (`MARK_END`).

measured_taken.description: `work_end - work_begin` — the measured taken-branch count of the window. The checker RECOMPUTES this from the two endpoints rather than believing it, and fails a record whose own field disagrees.

reported_taken.description: The retry count the payload reported in-band (`STXR` retries, seqlock retries). Zero for payloads with no reported term — and the checker fails a nonzero value on such a payload, since it would otherwise silently inflate the predicted count.

overflow.description: Overflow bookkeeping, when the sample armed one. `null` for a counting-mode sample (AA-1(a)/(b)).

step.description: Single-step measurement, for a stepped AA-2 sample; `null` for every non-stepped run. The AA-2 floor validates this structured evidence (PC advanced, exactly one instruction retired) rather than trusting the `exit_reason: debug` label. Unproduced pre-silicon: the stepping run path is arrival-day.

state_digest.description: Digest of the landed guest state, for replay-identity checks. Two samples that share (payload, scale, seed, condition, target) must carry the same digest — that is the same-seed determinism claim, and the checker enforces it from the records.

params_mode.description: The `PARAMS mode=` the guest itself printed — the in-band attestation that the harness really published the params page. The checker demands `managed`: a harness that forgot to publish it would otherwise run the smoke scale while the manifest claimed 1e8.

clockpage_mode.description: The `CLOCKPAGE mode=` the guest printed, for AA-5 runs. `null` elsewhere.

payload_status.description: The payload's own exit status. Nonzero means the payload's in-guest self-checks failed, and the sample is a failure however good its counts look.

payload.description: The oracle payload class (`oracle_model::Payload`). `ident` and `linux-guest` have no counting window; `linux-guest` is the AA-5 Linux VM the AA-6 determinism matrix requires (a full guest, not a bare-metal payload).

scale.description: The run scale (`oracle_model::Scale`). Note the serialized spellings are `s1e6`/`s1e7`/`s1e8` (serde kebab-case of the Rust variants), not `1e6`.

exit-reason.description: How this sample's `KVM_RUN` ended (`arm_harness::evidence::ExitReason`). Checked per record against `Mechanism::expected_exit_reason`, so a run that silently exercised the stock signal-kick path while claiming the patched exit cannot pass.

overflow-record.description: The overflow bookkeeping for one sample (`arm_harness::evidence::OverflowRecord`). §Evidence integrity #6: exactly-once is shown PER RECORD, never inferred from a total.

armed.description: Whether an overflow was armed for this sample.

deliveries.description: How many times the armed overflow was delivered. Must be exactly 1 — 0 is a lost PMI, >1 is a duplicate, and both are blocking.

advisory_exits.description: Exits that left KVM_RUN while armed but BEFORE the counter reached the target — the arm64 'any host IRQ' advisory exits (the patch fires KVM_EXIT_PREEMPT on any IRQ, not only the PMU overflow). Recorded, not hidden: 'the mechanism fires on unrelated IRQs' is a measured property of this silicon, not noise.

target.description: The work target the deadline was set for.

landed.description: The work count actually landed on. AA-3's acceptance is `work == target` on EVERY landing.

skid.description: `landed - target`. Negative means the landing was early (inside the skid margin, which is the design); positive means it OVERSHOT, which the late-only-stop contract forbids outright. The checker recomputes it from `landed` and `target` rather than believing the field.

landed_digest.description: Digest of the guest state AT THE LANDING, sampled before the guest resumed — the state AA-3's replay identity is about. The final state_digest can converge (two different landings reach the same exit state), so it cannot establish landing identity. Empty when nothing was delivered.

step-record.description: One single-step measurement (`arm_harness::evidence::StepRecord`), for a stepped AA-2 sample. AA-2 characterizes single-stepping, and its evidence is these measured numbers, not the `exit_reason: debug` label a rehashed run-set can flip in one byte. The AA-2 floor validates them: the PC must advance and exactly one instruction must retire. Unproduced pre-silicon — the stepping run path (KVM_SET_GUEST_DEBUG + the stepping loop) is arrival-day.

pc_after.description: The PC after the step — must differ from pc_before (a step advanced the guest).

insn_retired.description: Instructions retired by the step. AA-2's single-step semantics require exactly 1.

br_retired_delta.description: BR_RETIRED delta across the step, validated against `transition`: 0 for sequential, 1 for a taken branch, and a measured 0-or-1 for the exception/WFI/injection classes AA-2 characterizes.

step_digest.description: Digest of the guest state AT THE STEP MOMENT (not the exit sentinel). AA-2's acceptance is replay-identical stepped states across repeated inputs; replay identity compares this, because the final state_digest can converge.

step-transition.description: What kind of transition a single step made (`arm_harness::evidence::StepTransition`), recorded from the stepped opcode/trap syndrome rather than inferred from PC arithmetic — because `pc_after != pc_before + 4` cannot tell a retired taken branch from an exception transfer, and BR_RETIRED counts the former, not the latter. The AA-2 floor validates the delta against the class and requires the full matrix (including an LL/SC exclusive) to be covered.
